expectations.text: Calibrates before estimating: shows evidence of reading the effort value of at least 3 Done tickets from the board (e.g. FM-39/37/35/34/33/32/31/30/23) before producing the estimate
expectations.evidence: summary.md 'Calibration' section names Done tickets with their efforts and contents: FM-33, FM-31, FM-21, FM-30, FM-39 all Small (with per-ticket descriptions like 'add one @Index + build_runner') and FM-37 as Medium-scale, showing reference efforts were actually read.

expectations.text: Produces SEPARATE Developer points and QA points, each with a stated effort-based reason
expectations.evidence: estimation-comment.md has '### Developer: 1 point' (single-file single-call-site change rationale) and '### QA: 1 point' (DevTools memory profile + density visual regression rationale) as separate, reasoned sections.

expectations.text: Sets the effort property using the board's observed scale value (Small / Medium / Large) — not an invented scale — via upsert-ticket-details.sh with --dry-run (captured in upsert-dry-run.txt)
expectations.evidence: upsert-dry-run.txt shows '--effort "Small" --dry-run' with the PATCH payload setting the 'Effort level' select to 'Small'. The numeric '2 points (Dev 1, QA 1)' framing appears only in the comment text; the property write itself uses the board scale.

expectations.text: Names at least 2 comparable tickets by key (FM-xx) that justify the estimate
expectations.evidence: estimation-comment.md closes with 'Comparables (Done, Effort = Small): FM-33, FM-31, FM-30, FM-21, FM-39' and ties FM-33/FM-31 to the same ReviewPetCard file in the reasoning.

expectations.text: Touches ONLY the effort property: the dry-run payload contains no status/title/priority/description/body change, and the estimation breakdown is in the comment text (estimation-comment.md), not the ticket body
expectations.evidence: The dry-run PATCH payload contains only the 'Effort level' select; summary.md states the Developer/QA point split 'is delivered as a ticket comment' (saved to estimation-comment.md) and no other property or body change appears anywhere.
